{{Quote|Bilars are found on Mima II, a small, tropical world orbiting the orange sun Lar.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref>}}
'''Lar''' was an [[Color|orange]] [[Star/Legends|star]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]]. It was closely [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by the tropical [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mima II]]. Lar's [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] influence on Mima II caused the planet's tectonic plates to be drawn across its surface in various directions, resulting in the constant creation of new [[Geology|geological]] features there.

The [[electromagnetic radiation]] emitted by Lar also had the effect of abundant [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal life emerging on Mima II, which featured vast [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]]. The [[Bilar]]s, a [[Primate/Legends|primate]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|indigenous]] to the planet's jungles, had no need to shield themselves from Lar due to their native habitat offering protection from exposure to Lar's [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]].

==Description==
===Gravitational influence===
{{Quote|Due to Mima II's proximity to Lar, Lar's gravitational influence draws the planet's tectonic plates over its molten core in much the same way a standard planet's moon draws tides across its oceans. As Mima II orbits its star, the plates are pulled in different directions, constantly raising new mountain ranges, forming new land masses, opening new seas, creating new volcanoes, and so forth.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:DAelgoth-EAOC.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Lar was located in the D'Aelgoth sector of the Mid Rim.]]
Lar was an [[Color|orange]]<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> type K main sequence<ref name="K" /> [[Star/Legends|star]] located in the [[Lar system]],<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> a part of the [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around the star was occupied by the small, terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mima II]],<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> which was located close to Lar and completed an orbit around it in just 103 [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]].<ref name="GG42" /> The planet itself was circled by at least two [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Zorba" />

Due to the proximity of Mima II to Lar and the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] influence of the latter on the former, Mima II's tectonic plates were drawn across the planet's surface, similarly to some moons drawing tides across the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] on other planets. Depending on Mima II's position relative to Lar, the tectonic plates were pulled in various directions, and new land masses, [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] ranges, [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]], and [[Water/Legends|seas]] were being constantly created as a result.<ref name="GG42" />

===Effect on life===
{{Quote|All of this geological activity has released an abundance of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and many of the other building blocks of life. These, combined with the intense electromagnetic radiation from Lar, have produced a world that literally teems with life.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:Bilar2_GG4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The jungles of Mima II offered the sentient Bilars protection from exposure to Lar.]]
The high [[Geology|geological]] activity of Mima II released abundant amounts of [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]], [[Hydrogen/Legends|hydrogen]], [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]], and other substances vital for the development of living organisms.<ref name="GG42" /> The intense [[electromagnetic radiation]] emitted by Lar transformed these chemical elements into a variety of organic molecules, which led to an abundance of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal life on the planet.<ref name="GG41">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref>

An extremely [[Temperature/Legends|warm]], tropical world, Mima II's surface featured expansive, lush [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] that were inhabited by the [[Bilar]]s, a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Diet/Legends|herbivorous]] [[Primate/Legends|primates]].<ref name="GG42" /> Due to the Bilars living their whole lives in the shade of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] jungles, they had no need to shield themselves from direct exposure to Lar's [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]]. That fact, combined with the high temperatures of their native environment, resulted in the Bilars' skin being pink and completely [[Hair/Legends|hairless]].<ref name="GG41" />

==History==
The [[sentientologist]] [[Obo Rin]] mentioned Lar when detailing Mima II and its native Bilars in the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]'', a work in which he described those sentient species he considered to be the most important in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and of the most interest to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="GG42" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Gg4-1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Lar was introduced in ''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races''.]]
Lar was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook authored by [[Troy Denning]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG41" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Lar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the star Lar, in the "Imperial Core" [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Lar system in grid square L-17, relatively far from the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy's]] [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the system was situated in the D'Aelgoth sector.<ref name="EAOC" />

The [[1998]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' claims that the tectonic activity on Mima II was caused by a nearby ''planet'' named Lar,<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> which contradicts the first edition<ref name="GG41" /> and the [[1994]] [[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', both of which state that it was the star Lar that exerted its gravitational influence on Mima II.<ref name="GG42" /> This article assumes that ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' is in error.
